mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses the politics of international relations including state sovereignty, international institutions, and political authority (A).
Directly addresses the United Nations as an international organization for peacekeeping and collective security (A.2).
The legal framework governing treaties, sovereignty, and international law relating to wartime connects to outline B and E.2.e.
Directly addresses diplomacy, negotiation of international disputes, and public international conferences (C.2).
Peacekeeping, collective security, just war theory, and conflict resolution are central to B.1 and C.2.
Collective security, arms limitation, NATO, and the prevention of war between states are central to B.1 and B.3.
Nuclear weapons, deterrence, and arms limitation treaties are central to B.3.
The Cold War as a major period of international relations -- arms treaties, alliances, and geopolitics -- is central to outline A and B.3.
Geopolitical dimensions of international relations including territorial disputes and regional power dynamics connect to B.4.
Territorial treaties, border disputes, and the geopolitical significance of boundaries connect to B.4.
Modern warfare -- limited war, total war, guerrilla warfare, and psychological warfare -- connects to E.1 and E.2.d.
Civil wars and insurrections, including their causes, consequences, and transnational dimensions, connect to E.1.
Directly addresses military strategy as an element of the conduct of war (E.2.a).
Clausewitz's foundational theory of war, strategy, and the relationship between war and politics is central to E.2.a.
The technological development of weapons and military forces throughout history connects to E.3.
Religious wars as a kind of war and just war theory are relevant to E.1.
The historical development of organized military forces and ancient military strategy and tactics connect to E.2 and E.3.b.
A worldwide war involving military strategy, logistics, propaganda, and its aftermath in treaties is central to outline E and B.2.
A total world war involving military organization and strategy across ground, naval, and air forces is central to outline E.
A treaty terminating a major war and its consequences for international relations connect to B.2.
Air forces as a branch of the armed forces connect to E.3.c.iii.
Naval and amphibious forces as a branch of the armed forces connect to E.3.c.ii.
The historical development of organized military forces -- professional and volunteer soldiers -- connects to E.3.a and E.3.b.
Foreign policy aims and compatibility with national and humanitarian interests connect to C.1.
International humanitarian law, foreign aid, and humanitarian intervention connect to C.3 and E.2.e.
International law relating to military discipline and treatment of persons during wartime connects to E.2.e.
European federalism as a transnational political movement is relevant to A.1.
Nationalism as a transnational political movement driving Pan-national ideologies connects to A.1.
A regional conflict involving territorial disputes, diplomacy, and military force connects to B.4 and E.1.
A major regional war involving military strategy, tactics, logistics, and treaty settlements connects to E.2 and B.2.
A civil war with significant international dimensions and foreign intervention is relevant to E.1.
Religious wars as a major historical type of armed conflict connect to E.1.
A major civil war involving military strategy, organization, and the conduct of war connects to E.1 and E.2.
Crimes against humanity are relevant to the attempt to punish war crimes and crimes against peace (E.2.e).
Refugee crises resulting from wars and international conflicts are a recurring consequence throughout.
National liberation movements and their impact on international relations and sovereignty connect to A.1.
Emerging powers and their role in contemporary international organizations and diplomacy connect to A.2.
Terrorism as a challenge to international peace and security connects to E.1.
